operating_state: Total production reached record 1,792 MBOED, increasing 45 MBOED year-over-year driven by new wells online in Lower 48 unconventional plays and improved well performance across Delaware Basin, Eagle Ford, Midland Basin and Bakken. Production increases were partially offset by normal field decline and divestiture impacts from Indonesia and noncore assets.
cost_structure: Production and operating expenses increased primarily due to higher production volumes, increased well work activity and inflationary pressures. DD&A expenses rose driven by higher production volumes and elevated rates from reserve revisions reflecting higher operating expenses, partially offset by absence of DD&A from disposed assets and lower taxes from reduced commodity prices.
material_events: Acquired 30 percent equity interest in Port Arthur LNG joint venture upon Phase 1 FID, including 20-year agreement to purchase 5 MTPA of LNG offtake. Received Department of Interior Record of Decision approving Willow project development with three core pads, commencing construction. Announced plans to assume upstream operatorship of APLNG and acquire additional 2.49 percent shareholding interest subject to regulatory approvals. Accelerated GHG emissions intensity reduction target to 50-60 percent by 2030 from previous 40-50 percent target.
macro_exposures: Commodity prices decreased significantly with WTI averaging $76.13 per barrel down 19 percent and Henry Hub natural gas averaging $3.44 per MMBTU down 31 percent due to recession fears, mild winter weather and heavy global refinery maintenance. Bitumen prices declined 55 percent driven by weakening WTI and widening WCS differentials from reduced heavy oil demand. Russia-Ukraine conflict continues impacting global markets alongside potential OPEC production changes.
forward_outlook: Raised full-year production guidance midpoint by 10 MBOED to 1.78-1.80 MMBOED range with second-quarter production expected at 1.77-1.81 MMBOED. Reconfirmed 2023 planned return of capital to shareholders through three-tier framework. Operating plan capital expenditures expected between $10.7-11.3 billion focused on flexible short-cycle unconventional plays with access to domestic and export markets.
uncertainty_profile: APLNG operatorship transition and additional shareholding acquisition remain subject to Australian regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. Environmental accrual maintained for remediation activities across CERCLA sites. Evolving climate change regulations and GHG emissions laws could materially impact operations, with unresolved climate litigation ongoing. Venezuela government payment collection remains uncertain, and storage capacity constraints may require production curtailments.
